architect.description: Technical planning, spec writing, and DAG generation. Read-only — never edits files.

developer.description: Feature development, refactoring, debugging, and unit tests.

tester.description: Writes and improves tests. Maximises coverage, edge cases, and test clarity. Does not modify production code.

reviewer.description: Read-only code reviewer. Spots bugs, anti-patterns, and improvement opportunities. Produces structured review report.

security-auditor.description: Code security audit. Finds vulnerabilities, produces structured findings report. Never writes files.

documenter.description: Writes and updates technical documentation, READMEs, and API docs.

devops.description: CI/CD pipelines, Docker, infrastructure-as-code, environment configuration.

analyst.description: Read-only codebase analyst. Understands structure, dependencies, and patterns. Produces summaries and impact assessments.

nodes.task: Reply with exactly: ORCHESTRA_OK
Review the current codebase and write a concise technical spec for: {user_prompt}. Include: affected files, API changes, data model changes, implementation sub-tasks.

Audit the code changes in {coding_output}. Focus on: injection vulnerabilities, exposed secrets, broken access control, insecure dependencies. Return a structured findings report with an 'approved' boolean.
Based on {coding_output}, update any relevant documentation. Update the changelog. Do not modify source code.
Write a failing test that reproduces this bug: {user_prompt}. The test must fail on the current codebase. Save it and export its path.
Fix the bug so the test at {test_file} passes. Make the minimal change necessary. Do not refactor unrelated code.

Run `git diff --stat main | head -25` and `git status --short | head -25` to summarise changes vs main. List file names and the type of change (added/modified/deleted) with a one-line description per file. Do NOT read individual file contents. Keep total output under 200 words.
Analyse the codebase for: {user_prompt}. List every function, class, and module that lacks test coverage. Note edge cases and error paths that should be tested.
Plan a safe refactor for: {user_prompt}. Identify exactly which files and functions to change, what the new structure should be, and what must not change. List risks.
Verify the refactor in {refactor_output} matches the plan in {refactor_plan}. Confirm no behaviour changes were introduced. Flag any deviations.
Run the linter and formatter on the current branch changes and fix all auto-fixable issues. Do not change logic.
Run the full test suite. If any tests fail, fix them. Do not modify production code to make tests pass — fix the tests or flag the issue.
Write a pull request description for the current branch changes. Include: summary of changes, motivation, testing notes, and any breaking changes. Format as markdown.
Run a dependency audit: check for outdated packages and known vulnerabilities. Output a structured list of issues grouped by severity.
Apply the safe dependency updates from {dep_security}. Only update packages rated as must-fix. Run tests after.
